question: [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified + Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged maker atomically call setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) then take() then setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false) inside a single Midnight.multicall(), under the precondition that the maker controls their own authorization and the root covers a favorable offer, triggering the call sequence [multicall([setIsRootRatified(true), take(offer, ratifierData, units, ...), setIsRootRatified(false)])], violating the invariant that isRootRatified must reflect a durable maker commitment and not a transient per-transaction flag, causing scoped impact: offer ratification bypass — maker executes a take that would be rejected if ratification state were checked before and after the transaction? Proof idea: fuzz root values and offer parameters; assert that isRootRatified[maker][root] == false both before and after the multicall while the take succeeded inside it.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.setIsAuthorized + SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified + Midnight.take] Can an attacker who is authorized by a maker compose a multicall of [setIsAuthorized(ratifier, true, maker), setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true), take(offer, ...), setIsAuthorized(ratifier, false, maker), setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false)], under the precondition that the attacker holds isAuthorized[maker][attacker] == true, triggering a sequence where the ratifier is authorized and the root is ratified only within the transaction, violating the invariant that offer.ratifier must be persistently authorized by offer.maker at take time, causing scoped impact: gate bypass — a take executes under a ratifier authorization that does not persist? Proof idea: snapshot isAuthorized[maker][ratifier] and isRootRatified[maker][root] before and after; assert take succeeded while both were false outside the tx.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.setConsumed + Midnight.take] Can a taker compose a multicall of [take(offer, ..., units1), setConsumed(group, consumed_before, maker), take(offer, ..., units2)] where setConsumed resets the group counter between two takes of the same offer, under the precondition that the taker is authorized to call setConsumed on behalf of the maker (isAuthorized[maker][taker] == true), triggering double-fill of the same offer group beyond maxUnits or maxAssets, violating the invariant that consumed[maker][group] must be monotonically non-decreasing and never exceed offer.maxUnits or offer.maxAssets, causing scoped impact: offer overfill — maker's capital commitment is exceeded? Proof idea: assert consumed[maker][group] after multicall equals units1 + units2 but setConsumed reset it to consumed_before between the two takes.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: SetterRatifier.isRatified + Midnight.take (via multicall)] Can a taker use Midnight.multicall() to compose [take(offerA, ratifierData_A, ...), take(offerB, ratifierData_B, ...)] where both offers share the same SetterRatifier root but offerB was not intended to be ratified by the maker, under the precondition that the Merkle tree root covers offerA and the maker ratified that root, and offerB has a different tick or group but the same root hash passes isLeaf() for a different leafIndex, triggering a Merkle proof collision or second-preimage attack on HashLib.isLeaf(), violating the invariant that only offers explicitly included in the ratified tree can be taken, causing scoped impact: unauthorized offer execution — maker's ratified root authorizes unintended offers? Proof idea: construct two distinct offers whose hashOffer() values both satisfy isLeaf() for the same root with different leafIndex values.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.flashLoan + SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified + Midnight.take] Can an attacker use flashLoan() to receive tokens, inside the onFlashLoan callback call setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) on behalf of an authorized maker, then call take() to fill a favorable offer, then call setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false) to erase the ratification, all within a single transaction, under the precondition that the attacker is authorized by the maker (isAuthorized[maker][attacker] == true), triggering a sequence where the root is ratified only during the flash loan callback, violating the invariant that ratification must represent a persistent maker commitment, causing scoped impact: transient ratification exploit — offer executed under ephemeral root approval? Proof idea: assert isRootRatified[maker][root] == false before and after the transaction while the take event was emitted.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.take + Midnight.withdrawCollateral] Can a borrower compose a multicall of [take(sellOffer, ...) to increase debt, withdrawCollateral(market, index, maxAssets, borrower, receiver)] where the health check in withdrawCollateral uses the post-take debt but the collateral was not yet updated, under the precondition that the borrower's position is near the LLTV threshold and the sell take increases debt to exactly maxDebt, triggering a sequence where withdrawCollateral passes isHealthy() due to rounding in mulDivDown but the position is actually at or above the liquidation threshold, violating the invariant that withdrawCollateral must leave the borrower healthy, causing scoped impact: unauthorized collateral withdrawal — borrower extracts collateral while at or above debt threshold? Proof idea: fuzz collateral amounts and oracle prices near LLTV boundary; assert isHealthy() returns true after multicall but debt == maxDebt.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.take (seller) + Midnight.liquidate] Can a liquidator compose a multicall of [take(buyOffer, ...) to reduce borrower's debt below maxDebt, liquidate(market, collateralIndex, seizedAssets, 0, borrower, false, ...)] where the take in step 1 makes the borrower healthy but the liquidate in step 2 still executes because the health check in liquidate uses the pre-take state cached in the same block, under the precondition that the borrower is unhealthy before the multicall and the take reduces debt sufficiently, triggering liquidation of a position that is healthy after the take, violating the invariant that only unhealthy positions can be liquidated in normal mode, causing scoped impact: unauthorized seizure — healthy borrower's collateral is seized? Proof idea: assert originalDebt > maxDebt check in liquidate uses storage that was already updated by the preceding take in the same multicall.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.setIsAuthorized + SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified] Can an operator authorized by a maker compose a multicall of [setIsAuthorized(newOperator, true, maker), newOperator.setIsRootRatified(maker, maliciousRoot, true)] where the second call is made through a contract that calls setIsRootRatified using the freshly granted authorization, under the precondition that the operator can deploy a contract that calls setIsRootRatified in the same transaction, triggering a chain where the operator grants itself or another address the ability to ratify arbitrary roots for the maker, violating the invariant that only the maker or pre-existing authorized addresses can ratify roots, causing scoped impact: authorization chain abuse — attacker escalates to ratify arbitrary offer trees for the maker? Proof idea: trace isAuthorized[maker][newOperator] and isRootRatified[maker][maliciousRoot] state transitions across the multicall.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.take (with maker callback) + SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified] Can a maker's callback contract (offer.callback) call setIsRootRatified(maker, newRoot, true) during the onBuy or onSell callback triggered by a take, then a second take in the same multicall uses the newly ratified root to fill a different offer, under the precondition that the maker has a callback contract that calls setIsRootRatified and the multicall sequences [take(offer1, ...), take(offer2_using_newRoot, ...)], triggering ratification of a new root mid-transaction that enables a second take not intended by the maker at the start of the transaction, violating the invariant that ratified roots must be set before the transaction begins, causing scoped impact: callback-enabled root injection — maker's callback inadvertently enables additional offer fills? Proof idea: assert that newRoot was not ratified before the multicall and that take(offer2) succeeded using it.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.take + Midnight.repay] Can a borrower compose a multicall of [take(sellOffer, units, ...) to borrow, repay(market, units, borrower, callback, data)] where the repay callback (onRepay) calls setIsRootRatified to ratify a new root, then a third take in the same multicall uses that root, under the precondition that the borrower controls the repay callback contract and the multicall is [take(sell), repay(with_callback), take(using_new_root)], triggering a sequence where the repay callback injects a new ratified root mid-transaction enabling an additional take, violating the invariant that all ratified roots used in a transaction must be set before the transaction, causing scoped impact: repay callback root injection — additional unauthorized offer fills enabled mid-transaction? Proof idea: fuzz repay units and new root values; assert take(using_new_root) succeeds only because of the callback-injected ratification.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.updatePosition + Midnight.take] Can an attacker compose a multicall of [updatePosition(market, user), take(offer, ...)] where updatePosition applies a lossFactor slash to the user's credit before the take, and the take then uses the slashed credit to net against debt, under the precondition that a bad debt event occurred and the user has stale credit, triggering a sequence where the take's buyerCreditIncrease calculation uses the post-slash credit but the _updatePosition inside take re-applies the slash, causing double-slashing of the user's credit, violating the invariant that lossFactor must be applied exactly once per position update, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption — user's credit is slashed twice in one transaction? Proof idea: compare position.credit before multicall, after updatePosition, and after take; assert credit decreases by more than the correct slash amount.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget + SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker compose a transaction where MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget() is called with takes that use SetterRatifier, and between the collateral supply step and the take loop the attacker (as an authorized operator) calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false) via a separate call in the same block, under the precondition that the attacker is authorized by the maker and the bundler does not re-check ratification state between collateral supply and take, triggering a sequence where collateral is supplied but the take fails silently (try/catch), leaving the taker with supplied collateral but no debt reduction, violating the invariant that supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget must either complete the full sell or revert, causing scoped impact: fund freeze — collateral is locked in the protocol with no corresponding debt position? Proof idea: assert that after the bundler call, taker has collateral deposited but filledUnits < targetUnits and the function reverts with OutOfOffers.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.take (two offers, same group)] Can a taker compose a multicall of [take(offer1, ratifierData1, units1, ...), take(offer2, ratifierData2, units2, ...)] where offer1 and offer2 share the same group (offer.group) and the same maker, and both are in the same SetterRatifier root, under the precondition that consumed[maker][group] + units1 + units2 > offer.maxUnits but each individual take passes the ConsumedUnits check, triggering a sequence where the group budget is exceeded across two takes in the same multicall because the consumed state is updated atomically but the check is per-take, violating the invariant that the total fill across all offers in a group must not exceed the group budget, causing scoped impact: offer overfill — maker's total exposure exceeds their stated maximum? Proof idea: assert consumed[maker][group] after multicall equals units1 + units2 and exceeds offer.maxUnits.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.take + Midnight.withdraw] Can a lender compose a multicall of [take(buyOffer, units, ...) to gain credit, withdraw(market, units, lender, receiver)] where the withdraw uses the credit gained in the same multicall before any repayment has occurred, under the precondition that marketState.withdrawable is sufficient from prior repayments and the lender's new credit equals the withdrawn units, triggering a sequence where the lender gains credit and immediately withdraws it in the same transaction without any corresponding debt repayment in this transaction, violating the invariant that withdrawable must only increase via repay or liquidation and credit withdrawal must correspond to previously repaid assets, causing scoped impact: insolvency — lender withdraws assets that have not been repaid? Proof idea: assert that marketState.withdrawable decreases by units and the lender's credit decreases by units, but no repay event was emitted in the same multicall.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.touchMarket + Midnight.take] Can an attacker compose a multicall of [touchMarket(market_with_zero_settlement_fee), take(offer_in_that_market, ...)] where the market is created with zero default settlement fees in the same transaction as the take, under the precondition that defaultSettlementFeeCbp[loanToken] is all zeros and the attacker creates the market and immediately takes an offer, triggering a sequence where the settlement fee is zero for the take because the market was just created with zero fees, violating the invariant that settlement fees must be applied correctly at take time and cannot be gamed by market creation timing, causing scoped impact: fee underpayment — taker pays zero settlement fee by creating the market in the same multicall? Proof idea: compare claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] before and after the multicall; assert it did not increase despite a take occurring.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.liquidate + Midnight.take] Can a liquidator compose a multicall of [liquidate(market, collateralIndex, 0, 0, borrower, false, ...) to realize bad debt, take(offer_in_same_market, ...)] where the bad debt realization in step 1 increases lossFactor, and the take in step 2 uses the updated lossFactor to slash the buyer's credit before the take's _updatePosition, under the precondition that the buyer has existing credit and the bad debt realization changes lossFactor significantly, triggering a sequence where the buyer's credit is slashed by the new lossFactor during the take's _updatePosition call, causing the buyer to receive fewer net credit units than expected, violating the invariant that credit accounting must be consistent within a single transaction, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption — buyer's credit is unexpectedly slashed mid-multicall? Proof idea: assert buyer's effective credit after the take equals expected credit minus the slash from the bad debt realization.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified + Midnight.take (via Midnight.multicall, msg.sender context)] Can an attacker exploit the fact that Midnight.multicall() uses delegatecall, meaning msg.sender is preserved as the original caller throughout all sub-calls, to call setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) where maker == msg.sender (the attacker is the maker), then take an offer where offer.maker == attacker and offer.ratifier == SetterRatifier, under the precondition that the attacker creates a valid Merkle tree with a favorable offer and ratifies it in the same multicall, triggering a sequence where the attacker self-ratifies a root and immediately takes against it, violating the invariant that ratification must be a pre-committed state and not a same-transaction setup, causing scoped impact: self-ratification exploit — attacker creates and immediately executes offers with no prior commitment? Proof idea: assert that isRootRatified[attacker][root] was false before the multicall and the take succeeded.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.take (maturity boundary)] Can an attacker compose a multicall of [take(sellOffer, units, ...) to increase debt just before maturity, take(sellOffer2, units2, ...) to increase debt again] where block.timestamp == market.maturity - 1 for the first take and block.timestamp == market.maturity for the second take within the same block, under the precondition that the market maturity falls within the same block as the multicall, triggering a sequence where the first take passes the CannotIncreaseDebtPostMaturity check and the second fails, but the first take's debt increase is committed, violating the invariant that debt must not increase at or after maturity, causing scoped impact: maturity boundary debt increase — borrower increases debt at the exact maturity boundary? Proof idea: warp to market.maturity - 1, compose multicall with two sell takes, assert the second take reverts but the first committed debt.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.supplyCollateral + Midnight.take (sell)] Can a borrower compose a multicall of [supplyCollateral(market, index, assets, borrower), take(sellOffer, units, borrower, ...)] where the collateral supplied in step 1 is used to pass the health check at the end of the take in step 2, under the precondition that the borrower has insufficient collateral to borrow before the multicall, triggering a sequence where collateral is supplied and debt is increased atomically, violating the invariant that collateral must be present before debt is created (not just at the end of the take), causing scoped impact: undercollateralized borrow — borrower creates debt using collateral supplied in the same transaction? Proof idea: assert that isHealthy() would return false if the take were executed without the preceding supplyCollateral in the same multicall.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.setIsAuthorized + Midnight.take] Can an attacker compose a multicall of [setIsAuthorized(SetterRatifier, true, maker), take(offer_with_SetterRatifier, ...)] where the SetterRatifier is authorized as the ratifier for the maker only within the multicall, under the precondition that isAuthorized[maker][SetterRatifier] was false before the multicall and the attacker is authorized by the maker, triggering a sequence where the ratifier authorization check (isAuthorized[offer.maker][offer.ratifier]) passes only because of the setIsAuthorized call earlier in the same multicall, violating the invariant that ratifier authorization must be a persistent pre-existing state, causing scoped impact: transient ratifier authorization — offer executed under a ratifier that was not authorized before the transaction? Proof idea: assert isAuthorized[maker][SetterRatifier] == false before the multicall and the take succeeded.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.take + Midnight.setConsumed (cancel)] Can a maker compose a multicall of [take(offer, units, ...) as taker of their own group's offer, setConsumed(group, type(uint256).max, maker)] where the take fills the offer and setConsumed immediately cancels all remaining capacity, under the precondition that the maker is also the taker (which is blocked by SelfTake) or the maker uses an authorized operator as taker, triggering a sequence where the offer is filled and then the group is cancelled in the same transaction, violating the invariant that setConsumed must not be usable to retroactively alter the consumed state after a fill in the same transaction, causing scoped impact: offer state manipulation — consumed state is set to max after a fill, preventing future fills but not undoing the current fill? Proof idea: assert consumed[maker][group] == type(uint256).max after multicall while the take event shows a fill occurred.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.take (two markets, shared SetterRatifier root)] Can an attacker compose a multicall of [take(offer_market_A, ratifierData_with_root, units, ...), take(offer_market_B, same_ratifierData, units, ...)] where both offers are in different markets but the same SetterRatifier root covers both, under the precondition that the maker ratified a root containing offers for both markets and the attacker takes both in the same multicall, triggering a sequence where the group budget (consumed[maker][group]) is shared across markets and both takes increment it, violating the invariant that the group budget must correctly cap total exposure across all markets, causing scoped impact: cross-market overfill — maker's group budget is exceeded across two markets in one multicall? Proof idea: assert consumed[maker][group] after multicall equals units_A + units_B and exceeds the per-offer maxUnits.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.liquidate (bad debt) + Midnight.claimContinuousFee] Can an attacker compose a multicall of [liquidate(market, 0, 0, 0, borrower, false, ...) to realize bad debt and update lossFactor, claimContinuousFee(market, amount, receiver)] where the bad debt realization in step 1 reduces continuousFeeCredit via the lossFactor update, and the claimContinuousFee in step 2 uses the pre-update continuousFeeCredit value cached in memory, under the precondition that the feeClaimer is the attacker or the attacker can trigger this sequence, triggering a sequence where continuousFeeCredit is claimed at a higher value than it should be after the bad debt slash, violating the invariant that continuousFeeCredit must reflect the post-slash value before any claim, causing scoped impact: fee overclaim — feeClaimer extracts more continuous fee than is owed after bad debt? Proof idea: assert marketState.continuousFeeCredit after the multicall is negative (underflow) or that the claimed amount exceeds the post-slash continuousFeeCredit.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.take (reduceOnly offer) + Midnight.take (debt-increasing offer)] Can an attacker compose a multicall of [take(reduceOnlyOffer, units, ...) to reduce maker's credit, take(debtOffer, units, maker_as_seller, ...)] where the first take reduces the maker's credit to zero and the second take increases the maker's debt, under the precondition that the maker has credit and the reduceOnly offer is valid, triggering a sequence where the maker's credit is reduced to zero by the first take and then debt is created by the second take, violating the invariant that reduceOnly offers must not be composable with debt-increasing takes to create a net debt position for the maker, causing scoped impact: bad debt creation — maker ends up with debt but no credit after a reduceOnly + debt-increasing multicall? Proof idea: assert maker's position after multicall has debt > 0 and credit == 0.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.take (with liquidation lock) + Midnight.liquidate] Can an attacker compose a multicall of [take(offer_where_seller_is_borrower, ...) which sets liquidationLocked for the seller, liquidate(market, ..., borrower)] where the liquidate call in step 2 checks liquidationLocked and finds it true, under the precondition that the borrower is unhealthy and the attacker takes an offer where the borrower is the seller, triggering a sequence where the liquidation lock prevents liquidation of an unhealthy borrower within the same transaction as a take, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable (the lock is only valid during the take's callbacks, not after), causing scoped impact: liquidation DoS — unhealthy borrower cannot be liquidated in the same multicall as a take? Proof idea: assert liquidationLocked(id, borrower) == false after the take completes but before the liquidate call in the multicall.
